rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Requests For Admissions And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is entered by Judge Ross C. Moody. Respondents' Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) The Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions is GRANTED. Respondents served proper discovery and have shown that Petitioner failed to provide Code-compliant responses to RFPs number 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 21, 22, and 23, despite the Court's February 14, 2019 orders directing Petitioner to produce further complete responses to the discovery propounded in November 2018. (See CCP sections 2031.310, 2031.320). Petitioner has not justified his insufficient responses. Petitioner is ordered to provide the required responses and any responsive documents together with any relevant privilege log, within 10 days of the date notice of entry of this order is served upon him. Sanctions are awarded under CCP section 2031.310(h), based on the unsuccessful opposition to the motion and Petitioner's failure to show that imposition of the sanctions would be unjust. Petitioner shall pay to Respondents $2,920 within 10 days of service of notice of entry of this order. The Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Requests For Admissions And For Sanctions is GRANTED. Respondents served proper discovery and have shown that Petitioner failed to provide a Code-compliant response to RFA number 62. (See CCP section 2033.290.) Petitioner has not justified his insufficient response. Petitioner is ordered to provide the required response within 10 days of the date notice of entry of this order is served upon him. Sanctions are awarded under CCP section 2033.290(d), based on the unsuccessful opposition to the motion and Petitioner's failure to show that imposition of the sanctions would be unjust. Petitioner shall pay to Respondents an additional $2,920 within 10 days of service of notice of entry of this order. Total sanctions award against Petitioner and in favor of Respondents, is $5,840. Respondents shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on February 24, 2021. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925#, participant ID 0#, and passcode 722809#. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
